name: GPT-5.2
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); shipped as Instant/Thinking/Pro tiers
note: Adds GPT-5.2 Pro tier and new xhigh reasoning effort; SOTA at launch on GDPval, ARC-AGI-2, and long-context MRCRv2.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, GPT-5.2 Thinking; confirmed via marktechpost.com report of the launch page
AIME 2025, no tools, GPT-5.2 Thinking; confirmed
Humanity's Last Exam, GPT-5.2 Thinking; confirmed

GDPval 'wins or ties' score for GPT-5.2 Thinking, up from 38.8% for GPT-5. OpenAI's own launch page (openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-2/) returns 403 to automated fetch, so sourced to Simon Willison's write-up (fetched directly), which quotes OpenAI's official figure and matches the model's own 'note' field claiming SOTA on GDPval. Cross-corroborated by multiple other independent write-ups (vertu.com, turingcollege.com) all citing the same 70.9% figure.

SWE-Bench Pro score of 55.6% for GPT-5.2 (a new SOTA at launch), part of OpenAI's own launch benchmarks. Directly fetched datacamp.com's article which reports this figure; corroborated by multiple independent sources (Vellum's GPT-5.2 benchmarks write-up, a SWE-bench Pro leaderboard aggregator, and third-party commentary) all citing the identical 55.6% number.

FrontierMath Tier 1-3 (expert-level math), GPT-5.2 Thinking, maximum reasoning effort. OpenAI's own launch page (openai.com/index/gpt-5-2-for-science-and-math/) returns 403 to automated fetch, so sourced to MarkTechPost's direct report of the launch benchmarks; cross-corroborated by RD World Online and Vertu.com (vertu.com/ai-tools/gpt-5-2-benchmark-analysis-performance-comparison-vs-gpt-5-1-gemini-3-pro/), both independently citing the identical 40.3% figure. Represents a new SOTA at launch, ~10-point jump over GPT-5.1.
Not self-reported by OpenAI; this is an independent third-party eval.

verificationNotes:
Existence, Dec 11 2025 release, 400K context, and $1.75/$14 pricing confirmed directly via developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.2 and simonwillison.net/openai launch coverage. Six of seven benchmark rows (GPQA 92.4, SWE-bench 80, AIME 100, HLE 34.5, tau-bench 98.7, ARC-AGI-1 86.2, ARC-AGI-2 52.9) independently corroborated via marktechpost.com's direct report of the OpenAI launch page. Dropped the MMMLU row (89.6): could not find this figure for GPT-5.2 anywhere; found a different, unrelated 92.5% MMLU score and a 92.1% MGSM score instead, so 89.6 could not be confirmed and was removed.

2026-07-16: Researched the 5 missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu). Added livecodebench 88.9 from Epoch AI's independent eval suite for GPT-5.2 (relayed via themodelbeat.com/models/gpt-5-2, same source already used for gpt-5-nano.json's livecodebench/mmlu-pro rows in this corpus). For mmlu, math, and humaneval: checked OpenAI's own launch materials (blog returns 403, so cross-checked via 6 direct-fetch secondary sources that quote its comparison tables verbatim -- marktechpost.com, simonwillison.net, datacamp.com, vertu.com, deeplearning.ai's The Batch, and llm-stats.com) and independent trackers (Epoch AI/themodelbeat.com's 21-benchmark GPT-5.2 suite, Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index, Vals.ai's tracked-benchmark list) -- none report these three benchmarks for GPT-5.2. Cross-checked all 15 other GPT-5.x-family models already in this corpus (5, 5.1, 5.4/-mini/-nano/-pro, 5.5/-high/-xhigh, 5.6, codex, mini, nano, pro): zero carry mmlu/math/humaneval results, versus the older o1/o3/o4 family which routinely did -- a clean generational cutoff, confirming OpenAI and independent trackers stopped publishing these long-saturated legacy benchmarks starting with the GPT-5 generation rather than this being a research gap. For mmmu: base GPT-5 (Aug 2025) reported MMMU 84.2, but GPT-5.1 dropped it, and GPT-5.2's self-reported and independent (Epoch AI) materials only carry MMMU-Pro (~76%, per a direct read of vertu.com's OpenAI-sourced comparison chart) going forward -- OpenAI/trackers transitioned their multimodal benchmark of choice from MMMU to MMMU-Pro starting at GPT-5.1/5.2, so base MMMU was never published for GPT-5.2 and is correctly omitted rather than guessed. Disregarded automatio.ai's uncited figures (92.5% MMLU, 95.2% HumanEval, 76.2% LiveCodeBench, 86.5% MMMU, 94.3% MATH) as unreliable: the page cites no sources, and its LiveCodeBench figure directly contradicts the well-sourced Epoch AI number -- consistent with this corpus's pre-existing note about an unconfirmable 'unrelated 92.5% MMLU score' found in an earlier round.

2026-07-17: Second deep-pass research targeting the same 3 gaps (mmlu, math, humaneval), this time deliberately using source types the 2026-07-16 pass had NOT touched (that pass covered official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, and llm-stats.com). Checked all 6 requested new source types directly:

(1) Stanford HELM -- loaded crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest (Core Scenarios incl. MMLU-EM and MATH-Equivalent-CoT columns) directly: leaderboard is frozen at 2024-era models (top rows: GPT-4o 2024-08-06, DeepSeek v3, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Llama 3.x, Gemini 1.5/2.0); no GPT-5.x model of any kind appears. Loaded crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest (Core Scenarios: MMLU-Pro, GPQA, IFEval, WildBench, Omni-MATH): the newest OpenAI entry present is GPT-5.1 (2025-11-13) at row 34; GPT-5.2 has not been added to this leaderboard at all, and note this leaderboard's benchmarks (MMLU-Pro, Omni-MATH) are successor benchmarks distinct from the corpus's exact 'mmlu'/'math' (MATH-500) slugs anyway. Also checked HELM Instruct (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/instruct) -- stale at GPT-3.5-Turbo/Claude-v1.3/GPT-4-0314 era, no relevance.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar -- ran multiple targeted searches ('"GPT-5.2" MMLU site:arxiv.org', '"GPT-5.2" MATH benchmark arxiv baseline comparison', '"GPT-5.2" MMLU Semantic Scholar paper table comparison'). Directly fetched and read the two most promising hits, arxiv.org/pdf/2601.01330 ("Beyond Gemini-3-Pro: Revisiting LLM Routing and Aggregation at Scale") and arxiv.org/pdf/2602.14307 ("Benchmarking at the Edge of Comprehension") in full: both mention/cite GPT-5.2 but report zero MMLU/MATH/HumanEval scores for it -- their benchmark tables cover Gemini-3-Pro, Claude-Sonnet-4, DeepSeek-V3 and others, not GPT-5.2. A third paper (arxiv.org/pdf/2601.23169, "Names Don't Matter: Symbol-Invariant Transformer") does eval GPT-5.2 but only on propositional-logic/LTL tasks, not MMLU/MATH/HumanEval.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs -- checked evalplus.github.io (community-run HumanEval+/MBPP+ leaderboard, GitHub Pages-hosted from stanford-crfm-adjacent EvalPlus project). Confirmed stale: newest models are O1-Preview/O1-Mini (Sept 2024); zero GPT-5.x family entries of any kind, so no GPT-5.2 HumanEval(+) score exists there.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- loaded openrouter.ai/openai/gpt-5.2 directly (rendered page, not just metadata): its "Benchmarks" section sources exclusively from Artificial Analysis and reports GPQA Diamond (71.2), HLE (7.3), IFBench, tau2-Bench Telecom, AA-LCR, CritPt, SciCode, Terminal-Bench Hard, AA-Omniscience for the non-reasoning variant -- there is no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval field on this page for any model on OpenRouter. Loaded labs.scale.com (Scale AI SEAL leaderboard hub) directly: its 20+ leaderboards (DrugDiscoveryBench, SWE Atlas variants, HiL-Bench, MCP Atlas, SWE-Bench Pro, SciPredict, HLE, AudioMultiChallenge, Professional Reasoning Finance/Legal, Remote Labor Index, PropensityBench, VisualToolBench, MultiNRC, MultiChallenge, Fortress, MASK, EnigmaEval, VISTA, TutorBench) contain no MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval category whatsoever, for any model -- confirmed by reading the full leaderboard list text. Loaded livebench.ai (2026-06-25 release) directly: GPT-5.2 High IS present (Overall 74.6, Reasoning 83.2, Coding 76.1, Agentic Coding 50.3, Mathematics 93.2, Data Analysis 78.2, Language 79.8, Instruction Following 61.8, $0.234/task) and GPT-5.2 Codex also present (Overall 74.0) -- but LiveBench's "Mathematics"/"Coding" categories are its own proprietary contamination-free composite task sets, explicitly distinct from the corpus's MATH-500/HumanEval benchmark definitions, so this does not resolve the gaps despite GPT-5.2 being tracked. Loaded matharena.ai directly: GPT-5.2 (high) and GPT-5.2 (xhigh) both show 100.00% on AIME 2025 (independently corroborating the existing self-reported aime=100 entry via a third-party eval harness), but MathArena's benchmark suite is exclusively competition math (AIME, HMMT, BRUMO, SMT, CMIMC, USAMO, IMO, Putnam, Project Euler, ArXiv-sourced problems) and has no MATH (Hendrycks MATH-500) category, so no new score obtained for the 'math' slug.

(5) Non-English-language sources -- found and fetched a Japanese GPT-5.2 system-card summary blog (blog.beachside.dev/entry/2025/12/16/120000): confirms OpenAI's system card describes evaluating MMLU via professional human-translated test sets specifically for multilingual-capability/safety assessment, but explicitly states results showed "no significant difference from GPT-5-Thinking" and "no improvement in Japanese evaluation" without publishing any numeric percentage -- i.e., MMLU was used as an internal qualitative check, not published as a benchmark score. Loaded rank.opencompass.org.cn (leading Chinese LLM aggregator) directly: its current official closed-model leaderboard (updated 2026-05-13, 23 flagship models) does not include GPT-5.2 as a row at all -- it jumps directly from the base GPT-5 generation to GPT-5.4-2026-03-05; also note its columns (语言/知识/推理/数学/代码/智能体) are composite multi-benchmark category scores, not single raw MMLU/MATH/HumanEval figures, so even a GPT-5.2 row would not satisfy the gap.

(6) Sibling models -- cross-checked every comparison table encountered above that lists multiple GPT-5.x-family siblings (LiveBench: GPT-5.2, 5.2-Codex, 5.4, 5.5, 5.6 variants; OpenCompass: GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4-mini; Scale Labs: GPT-5.4, GPT-5.5; HELM Capabilities: GPT-5, GPT-5.1, GPT-5 mini/nano): none of these newer trackers publish a raw MMLU, MATH(-500), or HumanEval column for ANY GPT-5.x sibling, reinforcing the 2026-07-16 finding that this is an industry-wide generational cutoff (trackers have moved to MMLU-Pro, Omni-MATH, and LiveCodeBench-style contamination-resistant successors) rather than a GPT-5.2-specific reporting gap.

Conclusion: across all 6 requested new source types (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval-harness logs, other leaderboards/aggregators, non-English sources, sibling-model tables), zero credible MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval score exists for GPT-5.2 anywhere as of 2026-07-17. No number was invented or estimated. All three gaps remain correctly omitted.
